_comment: Malicious: CommandLineEventConsumer with encoded PowerShell — APT29-style persistence post-compromise
Name: WindowsUpdateCheck
_note: EventID=20 CommandLineEventConsumer with base64-encoded PowerShell. has_encode=true, has_b64=true, risk_score=95, confidence=HIGH

_comment: Malicious: CommandLineEventConsumer — LOLBin (cmd.exe) + temp path — ransomware affiliate beacon persistence pattern
Name: SystemPerformanceMonitor
_note: EventID=20 CommandLineEventConsumer pointing to dropper in ProgramData. has_lolbin=true, has_suspect_path=true, risk_score=90, confidence=HIGH

_comment: Malicious: ActiveScriptEventConsumer with VBScript — classic WMI persistence script; mshta or WScript variant common in APT41 tooling
Name: BITSJobHelper
Destination:
Set objShell = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
objShell.Run "mshta.exe vbscript:Execute(""CreateObject(""WScript.Shell"").Run Chr(99)&Chr(109)&Chr(100),0,True"")", 0, True
_note: EventID=20 ActiveScriptEventConsumer with VBScript launching mshta. has_lolbin=true, risk_score=75, confidence=MEDIUM. Service account User is additionally suspicious.

_comment: Malicious: FilterToConsumerBinding (Event 21) following consumer creation — binding activation, correlates with above consumer events
_note: EventID=21 binding follows Event 20 consumer creation 449ms later on same host. Correlate with Event 20 to confirm persistence is fully activated.
